title: erythroxylum striolatum
intro: Erythroxylum striolatum, also known as the Peruvian coca, is a plant species in the Erythroxylum genus. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Andean medicine, where it is valued for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The plant's leaves contain various alkaloids, including cocaine, which provides its medicinal properties, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and alleviating symptoms of altitude sickness. Erythroxylum striolatum can be prepared as a tea, powder, or extract, making it available in various herbal preparations, although its use is often strictly regulated due to the presence of cocaine.
